accumulated_interests.interest: Predictability and advance notice for financial requests
Documentation and accountability in expense tracking
Clear process boundaries that reduce conflict
Predictability in expense timing and amounts
Advance notice to avoid financial surprise

risk_signal_history.signal: Complaint-framing could signal blame pattern if not balanced
Opening with procedural grievance may indicate broader trust erosion
Has framed the issue entirely as procedural failure by Party B
No acknowledgment yet of Party B's stated concern

notes: Turn 1 with no prior party input - no behavioral signals to assess
Scaffold assessment of 'apparently_stable' and 'engaged_and_cooperative' cannot be verified without party participation
No relational pattern observable without at least one exchange
Baseline assumption: parties have chosen to participate, implying minimal threshold capacity, but this is procedural not psychological data
Party A has put a specific procedural complaint on the table with clear frustration signals ('should not appear weeks later')
Party B's complete silence is the most salient feature — we cannot assess whether this is consent, disagreement, emotional withdrawal, or simply normal turn-taking
The session risks framing as 'Party A's grievance against Party B' unless Party B is explicitly invited to provide context and their own perspective
The procedural complaint may be the tip of a larger issue (financial stress, trust breakdown, communication failure) — too early to assess scope
Both parties are engaged and substantive, but they are talking past each other at different levels of abstraction
Party A is focused on specific process failures; Party B is focused on structural power balance
Neither has acknowledged the other's frame as valid — they have not yet established that both concerns can coexist
Party B's non-response to timing/receipts concern is significant — could indicate disagreement, dismissal, or seeing it as tangential
The dynamic is cooperative in tone but structurally divergent — they need explicit bridging before option work will be productive
